case_title: Delayed process with burden shifted back to complainant
decision_trigger: The institution kept the matter active but left further progress dependent on the complainant.
recall_question: What happens when the practical burden of progress is shifted back onto the individual?
desired_outcome: A formal response, procedural clarification, and an evidence-based escalation path
rules_or_procedures: External escalation route after internal process delay
constraints: Need to maintain an accurate chronology

actors.name: Citizen complainant
actors.likely_behaviour: preserve records, seek procedural clarity, and escalate if necessary

actors.name: Complaints officer
actors.incentive_or_pressure: manage workload and contain procedural exposure
actors.likely_behaviour: respond narrowly, delay, or redirect responsibility

actors.name: Oversight reviewer
actors.role_in_case: assessing whether the complaint fits remit for external review
actors.incentive_or_pressure: remain within jurisdiction and evidential thresholds
actors.likely_behaviour: request a clear chronology and supporting records before progressing

evidence_bundle.label: Initial complaint email
evidence_bundle.relevance: Shows the original issue raised and the date the complaint process began

evidence_bundle.label: Acknowledgement reply
evidence_bundle.relevance: Confirms receipt of the complaint by the institution

evidence_bundle.label: Follow-up note
evidence_bundle.relevance: Records the absence of substantive response and captures the developing pattern

deadlines.label: Internal response deadline
deadlines.consequence: Missed deadline strengthens the case for external escalation

timeline.label: Initial complaint submitted
timeline.description: The complainant submitted the first formal complaint to the local authority.

timeline.label: Acknowledgement received
timeline.description: The institution acknowledged receipt but gave no substantive response.

timeline.label: Follow-up sent after no response
timeline.description: The complainant sent a follow-up requesting clarification and a formal response.

escalation_paths.name: Internal procedural follow-up
escalation_paths.trigger: No substantive response within the stated response window
escalation_paths.likely_response: A narrow procedural reply or further delay

escalation_paths.name: External oversight escalation
escalation_paths.trigger: Internal route appears stalled or exhausted
escalation_paths.likely_response: Request for chronology, complaint history, and evidence bundle

linked_cases.note: Earlier complaint involving delayed response by the same body

structural_insight: The institutional pattern appears to favour acknowledgement without timely substantive engagement.
personal_positioning: The complainant is attempting to remain accurate, measured, and procedurally grounded.
decision_note: Preserve the chronology, wait for the internal deadline to expire, then escalate with a tighter evidence bundle.
learning_capture: A structured timeline and evidence bundle make it easier to distinguish an isolated delay from a repeating institutional pattern.
